n.description: Number of DCs that must be opened

T_limit.description: Maximum allowed travel time from a DC to any hospital it serves

T.description: Travel time from candidate DC location i to hospital j

delta.description: Feasibility indicator: 1 if travel time from DC i to hospital j is within the limit, 0 otherwise

assumptions.description: Number of candidate DC locations is positive.
Number of hospital locations is positive.

code.python: assert all(T[i][j] >= 0 for i in range(nS) for j in range(nH))

assumptions.description: The travel time limit is non-negative.
The number of DCs to open does not exceed the number of candidate locations.
delta encodes feasibility: 1 iff travel time is within the limit.
assumptions.formulation: \delta_{ij} = 1 \iff T_{ij} \leq T_{\text{limit}}, \quad \delta_{ij} = 0 \iff T_{ij} > T_{\text{limit}} \quad \forall i \in \mathcal{S},\, j \in \mathcal{H}

code.python: assert all((delta[i][j] == 1) == (T[i][j] <= T_limit) for i in range(nS) for j in range(nH))

x.description: 1 if candidate DC location i is opened, 0 otherwise

y.description: 1 if hospital j is assigned to DC i, 0 otherwise

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(gp.quicksum(x[i] for i in range(nS)) == n)

constraints.description: Hospitals can only be allocated to active DCs, bounded by the number of feasibly reachable hospitals.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(delta[i][j] * y[i, j] for j in range(nH)) <= x[i] * sum(delta[i][j] for j in range(nH)) for i in range(nS))

constraints.description: Each hospital must be assigned to exactly one feasible DC.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(delta[i][j] * y[i, j] for i in range(nS)) == 1 for j in range(nH))

constraints.description: No allocation allowed if travel time between DC and hospital exceeds the limit.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(y[i, j] == 0 for i in range(nS) for j in range(nH) if delta[i][j] == 0)

objective.description: Minimize total drive time across all feasible hospital-DC assignments.

code.gurobipy:
model.setObjective(
    gp.quicksum(delta[i][j] * y[i, j] * T[i][j] for i in range(nS) for j in range(nH)),
    GRB.MINIMIZE
)

notes: The efficient formulation pre-computes a feasibility indicator $\delta_{ij}$ to avoid the need for big-M constraints.
We add an assumption that the feasibility indicator $\delta_{ij} \in \{0,1\}$ and $\delta_{ij} = 1$ if and only if $T_{ij} \leq T_{\text{limit}}$.
